2603.29953.title: Structured Intent as a Protocol-Like Communication Layer: Cross-Model Robustness, Framework Comparison, and the Weak-Model Compensation Effect
2603.29953.paper_summary: How reliably can structured intent representations preserve user goals across different AI models, languages, and prompting frameworks? Prior work showed that PPS (Prompt Protocol Specification), a 5W3H-based structured intent framework, improves goal alignment in Chinese and generalizes to English and Japanese. This paper extends that line of inquiry in three directions: cross-model robustness across Claude, GPT-4o, and Gemini 2.5 Pro; controlled comparison with CO-STAR and RISEN; and a user study (N=50) of AI-assisted intent expansion in ecologically valid settings. Across 3,240 model outputs (3 languages x 6 conditions x 3 models x 3 domains x 20 tasks), evaluated by an independent judge (DeepSeek-V3), we find that structured prompting substantially reduces cross-language score variance relative to unstructured baselines. The strongest structured conditions reduce cross-language sigma from 0.470 to about 0.020. We also observe a weak-model compensation pattern: the lowest-baseline model (Gemini) shows a much larger D-A gain (+1.006) than the strongest model (Claude, +0.217). Under the current evaluation resolution, 5W3H, CO-STAR, and RISEN achieve similarly high goal-alignment scores, suggesting that dimensional decomposition itself is an important active ingredient. In the user study, AI-expanded 5W3H prompts reduce interaction rounds by 60 percent and increase user satisfaction from 3.16 to 4.04. These findings support the practical value of structured intent representation as a robust, protocol-like communication layer for human-AI interaction.
2603.29953.paper_summary_zh: 如何可靠地讓結構化意圖表達在不同的 AI 模型、語言和提示框架中保留用戶目標？先前的研究顯示，基於 5W3H 的結構化意圖框架 PPS（提示協議規範）在中文中改善了目標對齊，並且可以推廣到英文和日文。本文在三個方向上擴展了這一研究方向：跨模型的穩健性，涵蓋 Claude、GPT-4o 和 Gemini 2.5 Pro；與 CO-STAR 和 RISEN 的受控比較；以及在生態有效環境中進行的 AI 輔助意圖擴展的用戶研究（N=50）。在 3,240 個模型輸出（3 種語言 x 6 種條件 x 3 種模型 x 3 種領域 x 20 個任務）中，由獨立評審（DeepSeek-V3）進行評估，我們發現結構化提示相對於非結構化基準顯著減少了跨語言得分方差。最強的結構化條件將跨語言的 sigma 從 0.470 降至約 0.020。我們還觀察到一種弱模型補償模式：基線最低的模型（Gemini）顯示出比最強模型（Claude，+0.217）更大的 D-A 增益（+1.006）。在當前的評估分辨率下，5W3H、CO-STAR 和 RISEN 都達到了相似的高目標對齊分數，這表明維度分解本身是一個重要的活性成分。在用戶研究中，AI 擴展的 5W3H 提示將互動回合減少了 60%，並將用戶滿意度從 3.16 提高到 4.04。這些發現支持結構化意圖表達作為人機互動中一種穩健的、類似協議的通信層的實際價值。
2603.29953.author: Peng Gang et.al.
